af_score_components.notes: afgong SQLite MCP server. Has MCP (quality 75). SQLite database interface. Documentation adequate. No auth required (local file-based). Rate limits not applicable (file I/O). Data operations focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local file-based (no network security). No auth (file permissions apply). SQL injection risks depend on implementation. Database file access control is critical. SQLite reliability (in-process). Not suitable for remote/untrusted environments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SQLite reliability (mature, battle-tested). File I/O patterns are deterministic. ACID guarantees. Write locks can block on contention. Suitable for embedded database operations. No SLA.

af_score_components.notes: afterpaths — unclear purpose from name. No MCP documented. Minimal documentation. Unknown use case. Cannot evaluate without context.
agenite — agent framework or library. No MCP documented. Documentation minimal. Purpose as agent tool unclear. Early stage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Early-stage project. No reliability guarantees evident.

af_score_components.notes: agenium — agent framework. No MCP documented. Documentation limited. Purpose not clearly defined. Early development stage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Early development. No reliability track record.

af_score_components.notes: agenium MCP server. Has MCP (quality 65). Part of agenium framework. Documentation minimal. No clear auth model. Rate limits unclear. Early stage.

security_score_components.security_notes: Early-stage framework. Security posture not yet defined. Likely not hardened for production.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: No reliability track record. Early development stage.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Analytics MCP. Has MCP (quality 70). Analytics data access for agents. Documentation moderate. Auth depends on analytics backend. Rate limits inherited. Data analysis focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Auth for analytics backend (strong model). Analytics data sensitive (user behaviors, patterns). Fine-grained access control possible. Credential management critical.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on analytics backend stability. No SLA for MCP wrapper. Suitable for data-driven agent automation.

af_score_components.notes: agent-arena — competition/testing platform for agents. No MCP documented. Documentation minimal. Purpose for competitive agent testing. Unclear API/integration model.

security_score_components.security_notes: Competitive environment — potential for malicious agents. Sandboxing critical. HTTPS likely. Auth required for arena participation. Risk model for untrusted code execution.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Competitive platform reliability varies. No SLA published. Community-driven infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: agent-bom — agent bill-of-materials or similar. No MCP documented. Documentation minimal. Purpose unclear. Early stage or partial project.
agent-breadcrumbs — agent tracking/tracing tool. No MCP documented. Documentation sparse. Likely for debugging agent execution. Purpose clear but implementation unclear.

security_score_components.security_notes: Tracing tools expose execution traces (could leak secrets if not careful). No documentation on secret scrubbing. Debug tools require careful handling.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Early-stage debugging tool. No SLA. Suitable for development only.

af_score_components.notes: Agent Deep Research MCP. Has MCP (quality 70). Research automation for agents. Documentation adequate. Auth varies by research sources. Rate limits from underlying APIs. Information gathering focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Multiple source integration (credential management). Research data public but aggregation reveals interests. Query pattern privacy concern. Suitable for research automation.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on multiple research sources. Aggregation adds failure modes. No SLA. Suitable for research agents.
